title: taraxacum radiosum
intro: Taraxacum radiosum, commonly known as the lesser dandelion or dandelion lettuce, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion genus, known for its edible and medicinal properties. The plant has been found to contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum radiosum have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering relief from symptoms such as bloating, eczema, and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum radiosum can be consumed in the form of tea, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall health and well-being by providing essential vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants.
